Description: Resets all settings to factory defaults when written. Must call commit for settings to persist over power cycle.

Timestamp in microseconds from sensor power.

Configures the response header for data returned over a wired connection. The only parameter is a  bitfield that determines which data is prepended to all data responses. The following bits are used: 
0x1: (1 byte) Success/Failure, with non-zero values representing failure. 
0x2: (4 bytes) Timestamp, in microseconds. 
0x4: (1 byte) Command echo—outputs the called command. 
0x8: (1 byte) Additive checksum over returned data, but not including response header. 
0x20: (4 bytes) Serial number. For the full 8 byte serial number the serial_number setting must be read.
0x40: (2 byte) Data length, returns the length of the requested data, not including response header.
Alias: header_status, header_timestamp, header_echo, header_checksum, header_serial, header_length

Description:
Alias setting to allow for easy setting of the status field in the header.
Status: Success/Failure, with non-zero values representing failure.

Alias setting to allow for easy setting of the timestamp field in the header.
Timestamp: time since sensor start in microseconds.

Alias setting to allow for easy setting of the command echo field in the header.
Command Echo: Outputs the called command.

Alias setting to allow for easy setting of the Checksum field in the header.
Checksum: Additive checksum over returned data, but not including response header.
Alias setting to allow for easy setting of the Serial Number field in the header.

Alias setting to allow for easy setting of the Data Length field in the header.
Data Length: Returns the length of the requested data, not including response header.
Returns a comma separated list of valid command numbers.

The speed that the sensors main processor is set to run at.
Valid speeds:
48MHz, 96MHz, 144MHz, 192MHz

Requires sensor restart to take effect.
Does not require commit to persist across power cycles.

Sets processor speed and component data rates based on presets.
0: Low Power - CPU = 48MHz, Max ODR = 500Hz
1: Normal - CPU = 96MHz, Max ODR = 1000Hz

3: Turbo - CPU = 192MHz, Max ODR = 2000Hz

Enables CPU power saving during idle time.

Configures data output slots for streaming mode. Accepts a list of up to 16 commands (comma separated U8). Every streaming iteration each command will be executed in the order they were entered. The output data will be put in the order they were called. Unfilled slots will be filled with 255 (0xFF)  for no command.
Time in microseconds between each streaming output. Values lower than 500 will be clamped to 500.
Output rate when streaming in hertz. Max value of 2000. Will clamp to closest microsecond value.
Length of time to stream in seconds. Duration of 0 will stream until stopped.
Length of time in seconds the sensor will wait after recieving the start streaming command to return the first data packet.

Controls how stream duration is handled.
0: Duration - controlled via the stream_duration setting
1: Count - controlled via the stream_count setting
A value greater than 1. Streaming will end after this many samples are output.
A comma separated list of all available streaming commands

Sets the method for which the sensor will obtain the magnetic reference vector (MRV):
0: Manual - Will only use the MRV currently stored in settings.
1: Semi-Auto - Will use the mrv set by user if it exists, else will attempt to automatically get a new MRV on startup.
2: Auto - Will always attempt to automatically get a new MRV on startup.
3: GPS - Will get MRV via GPS reading and WMM (world magnetic model), WIP.
The magnetic reference as a vector. This should be the expected reading of the magnetometer when the sensor is facing north.
Sets the magnetic reference vector via the WMM (world magnetic model) at the given GPS location.
Sets the magnetic reference vector via the expected magnetic dip.

Returns the thresholds used by motionless confidence factor for determining motion based on noise. All values are in units of Standard Deviations:
min: Completely motionless until above this
max: Completely in motion when above this
cap: Controls how much the noise term can grow. Influences settling time back to motionless.
The vector in sensor space that represents the "needle" on the compass when using the "Get Heading" command.
Helper to set heading_vector to one of 6 orthogonal directions. Valid values are x, y, z, -x, -y, -z

An offset in degrees and mode pair applied to the computed heading. Setting the offset to the magnetic declination will cause the heading to be relative to true north instead of magnetic north.

Valid Mode Values:
0 - Manual (offset is as specified by the user)
1 - Declination (offset is automatically set and updated via the WMM and GPS)

Automatically update the pts_estimator_scalars at runtime by using the incoming GPS data and predicted distance travelled. This value is between 0-25. 0 is disabled. See the EEPTS section for more information.

Experimental. Some heading methods may require a 180 degree heading flip when running is detected. This setting does that, but is typically not desired.

Default: 0

If enabled, will attempt to use hand specific settings when detected in the hand.

Default: 1

0 - None
1 - Gyro
2 - Orient

Angle (degrees) in range 0-360. If the difference between the predicted heading and corrected heading is greater then this, and the predicted heading has been consistently accuracte, update the corrected heading with the predicted.

Default: 5

If enabled, when updating the correct heading via the predicted heading, reset the consistent status.

Default: 0

If enabled, when updating the corrected heading via the predicted heading, also modify up to 10 steps in the past to also be affected by the modification.

Default: 1

Value in range 0-5.
If 0, disabled. 
Perform extra computing based on nearby steps to modify old steps motion types. This can result in correcting incorrect distance computations to more accurately compute the current location. The larger this value, the more motion averaging is done for accuracy, but the slower motion corrections will occur.

Value in the range of 1-25.

Backtracking on motion type will only occur after this many consectutive motion estimations are consistent.

Default: 5

Angle (degrees) in range 5-180.

When using orientation based heading methods, use this as the tilt angle at which the reference orientation may automatically change. Making this value too large can cause innaccurate headings, making it too small can cause inconsistent headings.

Default: 45
EEPTS version

The current date used to get information from the WMM. Set to current date for best results.

If enabled, GPS input to the system will be output from the system. Main reason to disable this is to observe peformance of the EEPTS system while still allowing GPS based calibration.

Default: 0x01

Angle in degrees. When transitioning from idle to motion, if the heading from pts_heading_mode is within this many degrees from the computed inital_heading_mode, the base heading will be considered valid.
Number of steps in a row that must be considered consistent before a new/corrected direction is considered valid.
Multiplier used to set the allowed error range based on the estimated change in heading angle. See EEPTS section for more information.

Sets the function of pins 5 and 6.
Mode:
1 - Uart (default)
4 - Orientation Level Interrupt (pin 5)
5 - Orientation Pulse Interrupt (pin 5)
7 - Button (Pin 5 and 6 become inputs, get status with the Get Button State command)
8 - Transaction Interrupts (See the "Transaction Protocol" "IRQ Mode" section for more information)

Sets the function of pins 2 and 3.
Mode:
2 - SPI (default)
3 - I2C
4 - Orientation Level Interrupt (pin 2)
5 - Orientation Pulse Interrupt (pin 2)
7 - Button (Pin 2 and 3 become inputs, get status with the Get Button State command)

Sets the baudrate of the sensors UART interface. Change will take effect immediately after the response is sent. 
Valid baudrates:
4800, 9600, 19200, 38400, 57600, 115200, 230400, 480800, 921600, 2000000, 4000000
Sets the sensors address used for communicating over I2C. Will not be applied until I2C/Sensor is reinitialized. For a list of valid addresses look at the I2C section of the manual.
Time in seconds that the power button needs to be held for the sensor to turn off. Set to -1 to disable power off via holding the button.

If 0, the sensor will power off when not connected to a wired connection. If 1, the sensor will stay powered via battery when not connected to a wired connection.
This setting is not commitable. If the sensor was powered via the button, will default to 1, else its initial value is determined by power_initial_hold_state.
The initial value power_hold_state gets set to when the sensor initializes.
Applies the settings contained in the <Root>:/CONFIG/sensor.cfg file.
Enable or disable the mass storage controller.
View the "Auto Mass Storage Control" section for details.

Configures data output slots for logging. Accepts a list of up to 16 commands (comma separated U8). Every log iteration each command will be executed in the order they were entered. The output data will be put in the order they were called. Unfilled slots will be filled with 255 (0xFF)  for no command.
Set the time between data logging packets in microseconds.
Set the data logging rate in samples per second (Hz).

Enum for the logging start event. 
0- Button
1- Accel Event Threshold
2- None/Command Only
3- On GPS Fix
4- On Power

Pass in any combination as a comma separated list.

Threshold value used for the Accel Event Threshold start event. Logging starts when the change in acceleration (g) magnitude within a 0.1 second window exceeds this value.

In general, smaller values will require less motion to start, and larger values will require more motion to start.

Enum for the logging stop event.
0- Button
1- Accel Event Threshold
2- Command Only
3- Duration
4- Capture Count
5- Period Count

Pass in any combination as a comma separated list.

Threshold value used for the Accel Event Threshold stop event. Logging stops when the change in acceleration magnitude within a 0.1 second window is continously beneath this value for the time specified by log_stop_motion_delay.

In general, larger values will stop more easily while experiencing larger motion, and smaller values will require the sensor to be more motionless to stop.
Time in seconds the change in acceleration magnitude within a 0.1 second window needs to be below the motion threshold for the stop event to trigger.
Value used for the Capture Count stop event. Logging stops when this number of samples have been gathered.
Value used for the Duration stop event. Logging stops when this duration, in seconds, has elapsed.
Value used for the Period Count stop event. Logging stops when this number of periods have been logged.

Enum for determining the method of data logging.
0: Continous - Single logging period for each capture session.
1: Periodic - Multiple logging periods for a capture session.
Amount of time each period will log while in periodic mode.
Time to wait between each capture period.

Base filename used for each data file. The final filename will be "{log_base_filename}{N}.[csv|bin]" where N is an incrementing file count and csv or bin is based on log_data_mode. N starts at 0 during a session for all logging modes, and will increase for each additional file when log_file_mode is set to "New" instead of "Append".

Enum for how new data is logged during logging sessions in periodic mode.
0: Append - Append data from subsequent perioids to a single capture session file.
1: New - Create a new log file for every period during the logging session.

Enum for determining ascii or binary logging.
1: Ascii 
2: Binary
If set to 1 a setting file will be created at the start of a capture session to capture the settings used during that capture session.
If set to 1, the response header will be included in the log results.

0: Session - Name folders based on an auto incrementing session index.
1: Date Time - Name folders based on current date time read from RTC.
If set to 1, the data being logged will be streamed out at the same time using the file streaming protocol.
If set to 1, the header will be prepended to each file stream packet while log_immediate_output is enabled.

Controls what protocol mode the immediate_output_header uses.
0: Match - uses the mode specified by the log_data_mode setting
1: Ascii
2: Binary

Timezone offset in hours in the range of -12 to +14

Changing the offset will modify the current reported time accordingly. Ensure offset is set before providing a time in that timezone for accuracy.
Typical charge rate in mA. Note that the set charge rate may differ from the actual charge rate to some degree. If different, a debug message will be generated when this is set.
The charge rate as a percentage of typical (0.0-1.0) and the temperature it occurs at in degrees celsius.
The charge rate as a percentage of typical (0.0-1.0) and the temperature it occurs at in degrees celsius.
The charge rate as a percentage of typical (0.0-1.0) and the temperature it occurs at in degrees celsius.

When going from hot to warm or warm to normal battery states, the temperature must fall this far below the threshold for the state change to occur. This is to prevent situations where the sensor flickers between states due to the sensor heating up while charging and then cooling when charging is shut off repeatedly, causing the temperature to border a threshold.
The onboard batteries max capacity in mAh. Returns 0 if unknown.
Advertised name of BLE enabled sensors. Max size 19 characters. Requires reboot for name to apply.

Returns the connection status of the BLE module.
0: Disconnected
1: Connected
-1: Not available
Disconnects from whatever BLE device is connected to the sensor.

If set to 1, the GPS will be in standby mode. If set to 0, the GPS will be active. 
Defaults to 0.
If set to 1, the led will appear green while the GPS has a fix.
Configures the periodic mode of the GPS. The GPS will be active for the first amount of time given (seconds), and then sleep for the second amount of time given (seconds), when the GPS has a fix.

Configures the periodic mode of the GPS. The GPS will be active for the first amount of time given (seconds), and then sleep for the second amount of time given (seconds), when the GPS does not have a fix.
When gps_standby is 0, and this is 1, periodic mode will be enabled. If gps_standby is 1, this setting does nothing.
The time interval, in seconds, that the RTC reading will be re-synced to the GPS reported time. When set, the RTC will sync to the GPS as soon as possible.

A value of 0 or 1. If enabled (1), the GPS will remain active, regardless of standby and periodic settings, until a time is obtained for the RTC to sync to. If disabled (0), the GPS will continue to operate as specified by the other settings, and the time will sync as soon as possible.
The time, in seconds, after not being enumerated/communicated with over USB, that the USB interface will disable itself.
